Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. The Mrs Fractal: Mirror, Rotate, Scale (2025) 35 upvotes, 3 comments
  2. Okmain: How to pick an OK main colour of an image 198 upvotes, 40 comments
  3. Can I run AI locally? 448 upvotes, 117 comments
  4. Gvisor on Raspbian 53 upvotes, 10 comments
  5. Removing recursion via explicit callstack simulation 16 upvotes, 1 comments
  6. Lost Doctor Who Episodes Found 130 upvotes, 36 comments
  7. Willingness to look stupid 687 upvotes, 237 comments
  8. The Accidental Room (2018) 14 upvotes, 1 comments
  9. The Wyden Siren Goes Off Again: We'll Be "Stunned" by NSA Under Section 702 190 upvotes, 65 comments
  10. Meta Platforms: Lobbying, dark money, and the App Store Accountability Act 1070 upvotes, 461 comments
  11. Executing programs inside transformers with exponentially faster inference 268 upvotes, 105 comments
  12. Using Thunderbird for RSS 7 upvotes, 0 comments
  13. Dijkstra's Crisis: The End of Algol and Beginning of Software Engineering (2010) [pdf] 55 upvotes, 18 comments
  14. Show HN: Channel Surfer – Watch YouTube like it’s cable TV 178 upvotes, 81 comments
  15. Hammerspoon 29 upvotes, 8 comments
  16. E2E encrypted messaging on Instagram will no longer be supported after 8 May 304 upvotes, 161 comments
  17. Bucketsquatting is (finally) dead 277 upvotes, 147 comments
  18. TUI Studio – visual terminal UI design tool 435 upvotes, 249 comments
  19. Show HN: What was the world listening to? Music charts, 20 countries (1940–2025) 90 upvotes, 42 comments
  20. Launch HN: Captain (YC W26) – Automated RAG for Files 34 upvotes, 13 comments
  21. My Life Got 100x Better When I Stopped Thinking About Google 5 upvotes, 0 comments
  22. The Bovadium Fragments: Together with The Origin of Bovadium 37 upvotes, 16 comments
  23. Show HN: Svglib a SVG parser and renderer for Windows 6 upvotes, 1 comments
  24. Qatar helium shutdown puts chip supply chain on a two-week clock 162 upvotes, 139 comments
  25. Your phone is an entire computer 86 upvotes, 77 comments
  26. Show HN: Context Gateway – Compress agent context before it hits the LLM 21 upvotes, 13 comments
  27. Launch HN: Spine Swarm (YC S23) – AI agents that collaborate on a visual canvas 68 upvotes, 59 comments
  28. John Carmack about open source and anti-AI activists 79 upvotes, 82 comments
  29. Parallels confirms MacBook Neo can run Windows in a virtual machine 70 upvotes, 99 comments
  30. Militaries are scrambling to create their own Starlink 38 upvotes, 53 comments

Last generated 3:30am, 14 March 2026. Source here.
